name: Information Security Policy
description: A policy framework defining information security objectives, responsibilities, and governance aligned with ISO 27001 and business strategy.

name: Information Security Roles and Responsibilities
description: Assignment of security roles including CISO, security architects, and operational owners across all business units and IT functions.

name: Segregation of Duties
description: Conflicting duties and areas of responsibility are segregated to reduce opportunities for unauthorized or unintentional modification of systems and data.

name: Supplier Security
description: Security requirements are defined and enforced for all third-party suppliers, including cloud service providers and software vendors, via contractual agreements.

name: Asset Management
description: All information assets are inventoried, classified, and assigned an owner with accountability for their protection throughout the asset lifecycle.

name: Risk Assessment and Treatment
description: Regular risk assessments are conducted using a defined methodology. Identified risks are treated via mitigation, transfer, acceptance, or avoidance.

name: Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery
description: Business continuity plans and IT disaster recovery procedures are documented, tested, and reviewed to ensure timely恢复 of critical services.

name: Incident Management
description: A structured incident response process covers detection, reporting, triage, containment, eradication, and post-incident review for all security events.

name: Compliance with Legal and Regulatory Requirements
description: All information systems comply with applicable legal, regulatory, and contractual requirements including GDPR, SOX, and sector-specific regulations.

name: Information Security Awareness and Training
description: All employees and contractors receive mandatory security awareness training upon onboarding and annually thereafter, with role-specific advanced modules.

name: Access Control Policy
description: Access to systems and data is governed by role-based access control with least-privilege principles, reviewed quarterly and revoked promptly on role change or offboarding.

name: Multi-Factor Authentication
description: Multi-factor authentication is required for all administrative access, remote access, and access to systems processing sensitive or regulated data.

name: Data Encryption at Rest
description: All sensitive data stored in databases, file systems, and cloud storage is encrypted using AES-256 or equivalent approved cryptographic algorithms.

name: Data Encryption in Transit
description: All data transmitted over untrusted networks is encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher with strong cipher suites and valid certificates.

name: Vulnerability Management
description: Automated vulnerability scanning is performed weekly on all infrastructure and application components. Critical vulnerabilities are remediated within 48 hours.

name: Secure Software Development Lifecycle
description: Security is integrated into every phase of the SDLC including threat modeling, static analysis, dependency scanning, and security acceptance testing.

name: Network Segmentation
description: Production, development, and corporate networks are segmented with firewalls and strict ACLs. OT networks are isolated from IT networks via unidirectional gateways.

name: Logging and Monitoring
description: Security-relevant events from systems, networks, and applications are collected in a SIEM platform with real-time alerting and a minimum retention period of 12 months.

name: Penetration Testing
description: External and internal penetration tests are conducted annually by accredited third parties, with all high-severity findings remediated before re-test.

name: Physical Security Perimeters
description: Data centres and critical infrastructure are protected by multi-zone physical access controls including biometric authentication, CCTV surveillance, and mantrap entry.

name: Clear Desk and Clear Screen
description: All personnel must lock workstations when unattended and clear desks of sensitive documents at end of day to prevent unauthorized access or information leakage.

name: Secure Disposal and Reuse of Equipment
description: All storage media and IT equipment are sanitized or physically destroyed before disposal or reuse, following NIST SP 800-88 guidelines.

name: Change Management
description: All changes to production systems, applications, and infrastructure follow a documented change management process with approval, testing, and rollback planning.

name: Endpoint Protection
description: All endpoints are protected by approved anti-malware, host intrusion prevention, and are enrolled in a device management platform enforcing security baselines.

name: Identity and Access Management
description: Centralized identity management with automated provisioning and de-provisioning, SSO integration, and periodic access certification for all enterprise systems.
